questions.question: Differentiate between distance and displacement.
questions.answer: Distance is the total path travelled, while displacement is the shortest distance between initial and final positions.

questions.question: Give one situation where displacement is zero but distance is not zero.
questions.answer: When a person walks in a circle and returns to the starting point.

questions.question: Define average speed.
questions.answer: Average speed is the total distance travelled divided by total time taken.

questions.question: Why is velocity considered a vector quantity?
questions.answer: Because velocity has both magnitude and direction.

questions.question: What happens to velocity when the direction of motion changes?
questions.answer: Velocity changes because it depends on direction.

questions.question: Define acceleration.
questions.answer: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with time.

questions.question: What does negative acceleration indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates a decrease in velocity or retardation.

questions.question: What type of motion occurs when velocity changes continuously?
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of velocity?
questions.answer: Metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: What does the slope of a distance-time graph indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates the speed of the object.

questions.question: What does the slope of a velocity-time graph represent?
questions.answer: Acceleration.

questions.question: What does a horizontal line in a velocity-time graph represent?
questions.answer: Uniform velocity.

questions.question: What does the area under a velocity-time graph represent?
questions.answer: Displacement.

questions.question: Write the formula for acceleration.
questions.answer: a = (v − u) / t

questions.question: What do the symbols u, v, a and t represent in motion equations?
questions.answer: u = initial velocity, v = final velocity, a = acceleration, t = time.

questions.question: Name the three equations of motion.
questions.answer: v = u + at, s = ut + ½at², v² − u² = 2as.

questions.question: Under what condition are the equations of motion applicable?
questions.answer: When acceleration is uniform.

questions.question: Give one example of uniformly accelerated motion.
questions.answer: A freely falling body.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of acceleration?
questions.answer: Metre per second squared (m/s²).

questions.question: Why is circular motion considered accelerated motion?
questions.answer: Because the direction of velocity continuously changes.

questions.question: Define uniform circular motion.
questions.answer: Motion along a circular path with constant speed.

questions.question: Give one example of uniform circular motion.
questions.answer: The motion of the Earth around the Sun.

questions.question: What instrument measures the distance travelled by a vehicle?
questions.answer: Odometer.

questions.question: What instrument measures the speed of a vehicle?
questions.answer: Speedometer.

questions.question: What is meant by retardation?
questions.answer: Retardation is negative acceleration that decreases velocity.
